This site has been developed to help you localise the Paratext videos. Notes for the videos are grouped by stages. Introduction (0.x) Stage 1 (1.x - 1A.x) Stage 2 (2.x) Stage 3 (3.x) Stages 4-6 (4.x, 5.x, 6.x) For each video, it describes the resources available advice on the choice of project for each video notes on any preparation need on the project any screenshots, pdfs etc notes on any potential problems Choosing a suitable project is important. If you do not have a suitable local project, sample projects available for you to use. You can either restore a backup or copy the MTT4 project Parated backups of a number of projects are available on the Google Drive in the resource folder Copies of the MTT4 project folder at various stages are also available at various saved with examples prepared. attention These folders are copies of the contents of MTT4 folder in My Paratext 8 Projects folder. To use them you copy the contents to the folder in your My Paratext 8 Projects folder. Please do not send/receive. Copy and paste the folders instead.
There are at least 24 introductory videos. These notes have been prepared to help you localise the videos. astuce Most if not all of these videos can be done with any project.
These two videos (0.01a+0.01b) have been split. See navigation below.
Prep make sure set to standard menus (not full menus) this video uses colloquial American English. (Hamburger menu!)
Prep make sure you have several \r reference lines add a reference to a footnote

Resources Video: Overvew: English srt: Project plan: 1:27, 2:07 This is a detailed video showing most of the functions. It is best to leave it until last, That way you will have have prepared suitable projects and may have footage to reuse. Project Plan Notes. Resources and several Enhanced Resources Paratext Live. Parallel Passages tool Biblical Terms tool. Spellchecking - Wordlst Back translation for the consultant, and/or prepare a more Literal back translation using the interlinearisation tool

Resources: Actions.srtOverview You can use any project for this video. You need to decide which language to start with. Since Paratext asks you to choose the interface language on installation, your viewers may already be in your language. So it may be better to start in your language and then change to English.
documents.s: # 6 -	0.2.1a	How To Change The User Interface Language (0.2.1a)

documents.t: Resources: Actions.srtOpening screen: Mat, with proj + 2 res you need permission to edit Matthew but not Mark you will use the Open menu (so check which filters are chosen) you will Close Paratext restart from Desktop (so cleanup your desktop!)
documents.s: # 7 -	0.2.1b	The Basics Of Projects And Resources (0.2.1d)

documents.t: Resources: Overview[Action.srt] Opening screen: Mat 1:6 project on left, 2nd col 3 res/res res Biblical terms Project: any??

First Good arrangement of windows Mat 1: Windows open: project, several resources (tabs and pane), biblical terms renderings. two items in Autohide (e.g. dictionary) one floating window (? greek text) Second Messy arrangement of windows 5 windows in columns Project, dictionary, two resources, notes list The video starts with a good layout (which is different to the final layout).But it is possible to start filming from the messy layout and then paste the good layout to the beginning.

Resources: Opening Screen: Changes in latest version There are now 2 ways to access Text collection in P9.3 Video starts with a text collection already open, then later closes it and shows how to open. Prep go to Open and check that Resources and Source language texts are the only ones checked. choose several resources to add to the text collection
documents.s: # 10 - 0.2.2a	How To Open And Modify A Text Collection (0.2.2a)

documents.t: Opening screen: Heb 10:1 with several resources open Prep Your project needs to have both OT and NT books you need a NT book with an OT quotation, e.g. Heb 10 + Jer 31:51 or Psa 8:3 and Mat 21.6 you need to open and drag several panes change scroll groups
documents.s: # 11 - 0.2.3a	How To Control Which Windows Scroll Together (0.2.3a)

documents.t: Resources Opening screen: Recording area: You will need to capture the Keyman icons on the taskbar Annotation text to translate: This computer and this user ONLY Prep You need at least two projects, one with special characters and one without you need the Keyman Keyboard for the project with special characters Screenshot of Keyman configuration showing that you have a keyboard installed
documents.s: # 12 - 0.2.3b	How To Set The Default Keyboard For A Project (0.2.3b)

documents.t: Resources Opening screen: Mat 1:1, project + 3 resources/ notes, dictionary in autohide, Prep choose a 4th resource to change to, make sure you have a saved layout, other windows to have open: Biblical terms windows, enhanced resource
documents.s: # 13 - 0.2.3c	How To Swap A Text In A Window (0.2.3c)

documents.t: Resources Recording screen: you need to include the taskbar Opening screen: Continues from 0.2.1d How To Arrange Windows Title slides to translate Tip1: How to swap windows Tip 2: How to undo moving a window Tip 3: How to unpin floating windows Tip 4: How to widen the autohide column Tip 5: How to undo closing a window Summary Prep Title screen from previous video "How to Arrange Windows" screen shots of Paratext menu showing undo tab move, undo tab close a variety of resources

Resources Opening screen: screenshot of GLO in preview Prep Projects: need to have the XXA... books, also need a resources with Deuterocanonical books Extra text to translate: Preface, Front matter, Back matter, Glossary, Topical index, Concordance, Introduction, Names Index Deuterocanonical books Screenshot of preview view of a glossary in your UI language.
documents.s: # 15 - 0.2.3e	How To View Other Bible Related Texts In Paratext (0.2.3e)

documents.t: Resources: Opening screen: Prep Make sure project has at least: Eph, Mat, Rom, Jhn, Plus two more books which have other books with similar names/titles Judges and Jude Philemon and Phillipians

Resources Prep several photos at start (check OK as is?) Dialog - translate text text to translate on animation (team work image - in English or find equivalent) setup projects to send/receive setup USB, Network drive and Chorus hub setup users roles and permissions - need administrator, team members, consultant, observer project plan with several tasks in stage 1 assigned, with drafting split between two users screen shots of chats/WhatsApp at end (text translated)

documents.t: Resources Prep text to translate in animation chat text make sure one project available on Internet is not on computer so it can received as New s/r same project to USB key and ChorusHub and Network drive you will need to delete the project in between

documents.s: # 76 - 3.2a	Back Translation: What is it and What Makes a Good One? (3.2a)

documents.t: Some of the errors are difficult to reproduce. The ! relates to a problem with verse number in in the translation project (not the back translation)
documents.s: # 79 - 3.2d	Back Translation: How the Status Boxes Help (3.2d)

documents.t: For this video, you need to know the glosses of several words/phrases.
documents.s: # 81 - 3.3	How and When to Use the Interlinearizer (3.3)

documents.t: Resources: Overview Actions Project: the mixed capitalisation check needs a language with lower-case prefixes on capitalised words. This MTT4 project is available. To use it download the folder and copy the contents in My Paratext 8 projects\MTT4. Opening screen ...

Opening screen: Mrk 5:4 Prep: several repeated words in inventory, at least one error choose two examples of repeated words in your languages e.g. English very very, and that that Confirm that repeated words check is in the project plan (normally added after stage 2) confirm that check has several issues you will mark one as invalid then correct some with the A&P and others with the menu.

Opening screen: Mat 1:6 Assignments and progress window Prep: choose a valid repeated word in your language (e.g. English had had) errors - repeated words, capitalisation, spelling, unmatched pairs of punctuation text to translate: Callout with a valid sentence with repeated word e.g. English 'He had had enough of their squabbling.' Callout with corrected sentence. Callout with an invalid sentence with repeated word e.g. English 'When they had had gone ...' Callout with corrected sentence. an invalid sentence with wrong homonym. e.g. 'He who has ears let hymn here.' Callout 'Deny is just to be used for exceptions which cannot be handled some other way.' (=last sentence of script) repeated word several times (e.g a word in a psalm) most of the errors denied.

Resources Opening screen: Luk 1:4, proj/res Project plan tasks+checks assigned to current user in Luk + Act; also in Next priority tasks in Jhn, some blocked by other user tasks in Act Green/Red striped current & next priority + blocked tasks 2:31 plus another project without a plan 0:39 Prep 2 users Screenshot of user registration
documents.s: # 29 - 1.1	How To View Tasks Assigned To You (1.1)

documents.t: Opening screen: Rom 1:1 Proj + 2 res

Prep project with plan 0:22 My Tasks - current priority then current book, `0:40 setup required, 1:44 close window work on CV errors uses wordlist to show need to fix cv errors. Choose a verse with a word that appears several times. Make a CV error so that one doesn't show up. (i.e. duplicated verse) CV, Markers and Characters all showing issues (so already run character inventory) Some errors (cv, markers chars) and chapters missing, "setup required" for Punct, Capital, Repeated words (so needs invalid entries in inventories) project without plan

Opening screen: Mrk 1:1 My tasks JHN: several tasks in progress, some waiting, errors in checks Prep Project plan with tasks assigned (some not complete) (see beginning of Overview) variety of tasks to mark as complete: project, chapters - both next chapter (+) and specfic chapters ...
astuce The video was done in an earlier version. To get the dropdown list to merge conflicts you need to change a verse (not the introduction as in the video.)
These videos add entries and definitions in English. It needs to be clear that it is normally in the vernacualar language.
This video shows English examples so users can understand the contents of the markers.

Prep Project plan Stage 2 checks Setup required for characters in one book (that is lots not marked as valid or invalid). more invalid characters in another book. languauge settings dialog (Menu, Paratext settings, Language settings) screen shot of video 1.4 on vimeo

Opening screen: Punctuation inventory dialog on screen (examples of valid, invalid and unknown visible) then transition to project Mat 1:15 Prep Project plan: 0:36 "setup required" for puncuation check (that is lots not marked as valid invalid) passage with examples of punctuation isolated, word medial and word final find other books which show "setup required" and others showing number of issues examples of same punctuation mark in list in different contexts (final, isolated ..) examples of both contexts but only one valid screen shot of video 1.4 (same as previous video)

Opening screen: Luk 1:1 proj//resources This is a difficult video to record. It is possible to use the English footage and add various screenshots and recordings in your language. Prep Annotations Consultant Check each User name Screen sharing Spelling Status sent Manuel's new note Isabella's new renderings bili spelled correctly bili spelled incorrectly Paratext Live local network, offline WiFi router, or hotspot with mobile data disabled. Manuel continues to edit chapter 3 Manuel's edit is visible "I'm marking bili as correctly spelled" Oh, are you sure it's correct?" screen shot of second user waiting to join choose text to edit conflict note File Explorer window open "My Paratext 8 Projects"
Prep text: Send/Receive Chorus Hub website - software.sil.org/chorushub Task manager: ChorusHub (showing background process)

Opening screen: Mat 1:0 showing: \h \toc1 \toc2 \toc3 \mt2 \mt1 Prep screenshot of PDF (showing how printed) text to translate: TOC = Table of Contents resources with different forms of \mt, \mt1, \mt2 + mt1 choose a book to create screenshot of book names tab translate text of Annotation toc1 = long toc2 = short toc3 = abbreviation

Prep Opening screen: Mat 1:1 proj/results/enh res Choose a word to Find and replacement text to type annotation text to translate: Inactive Window Active Window Excludes - Footnotes, Subheadings - Introductions
Prep Opening screen: Parallel passages tool Mat 14:1 current book. (Proj+1 res (no greek))

Prep Approve many rows (e.g. Mat 19.30 + Mat 20.16) Edit a verse for at least five (5) rows (e.g Mat 19.30) Project plan - shows several issues Uses Gal 1.5 and Mat 1.1 Need to have several verses untranslated
documents.s: # 95 - 6.1b	Parallel Passages Tool: Filters and Changed Text (6.1b)

documents.t: Opening screen: Mat 13:13 Proj/Enh res/Text col Text to translate: Slide: Paratext has a SET of Spelling Tools Slide: Approve Spelling of Common Words Spell Check Current Book Spell Check Common Typos Slide: Wordlist Tool Prep identify most frequent word count to approve ...

Text to translate: (similar to previous) Paratext has a SET of tools Spelling Tools Slide: Approve Spelling of Common Words Spell Check Current Book Spell Check Common Typos Opening screen: Mat 1:1 Proj+bibl terms/Res+Text col. Prep: identify words to correct... To do NEEDS more explanation on choice of errors.

Opening screen Mat 14:36. Footage showing earlier corrections. (1:51) Then Wordlist tool on screen astuce This tool makes use of previous corrections to identify other corrections. To get Paratext to remember the correction it must be made in the wordlist (not manual corrections). Prep: make a correction in the wordlist that can occur in other words. :::To do find examples to suggest :::
Prep: several capitalised words, a proper name, one occurs once, occurs both initially and medially Opening screen wordlist Mat 5:45

The English videos use a back translation (into an european language) as the translation project so that users will understand to process. But it needs to be clear that translators need to use their vernacular language project and do a back translation into your localised language.

one that is reconisable/vaguely understandable by the team if at all possible make sure you have copyright permission to use will need other projects for specific videos (e.g. mixed capitalisation, interlinearisation)
For each video there is at least: zip file of the archived English Camtasia file. overvew PDF actions.srt file (optional)
Some projects need either specific examples addition of mistakes to correct addition of users setup tasks/assignments to project plan inventories ...
